methodsAttempted.query: site:wsu.edu Washington State University generative AI policy students faculty academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official Provost Office AI guidance pages, an Office of Research generative AI guidance page, and one department-level AI policy page.
Official Provost AI section pages were fetched for course AI expectations, AI-related policy cross references, and academic misconduct reporting.
Official Office of Research generative AI guidance was fetched for research-scoped claims.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found WSU Provost Office guidance on course AI expectations, AI-related policies, and misconduct reporting, plus Office of Research guidance for generative AI in research. No single central binding university-wide generative AI policy was found; claims are scoped to the official guidance or policy references that directly support them.

stopConditions: Stop after verified official Provost and Office of Research sources provide claim-level evidence.
Do not use department-only pages for university-wide claims.
Do not use third-party summaries or external examples as WSU policy evidence.

Detecting and Reporting Misconduct Related to Generative AI
WSU Policies Relating to Artificial Intelligence

artifacts.claimText: WSU Provost guidance says instructors may allow or prohibit generative AI tools for their courses, with an obligation to communicate the course AI policy clearly to enrolled students.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a guidance-scoped claim tied directly to the WSU Provost course AI expectations page.
Approved because the source explicitly states that every instructor is expected to set clear expectations and must indicate course AI policy in every syllabus.
Approved as a narrow student-expectation claim from the Provost guidance; it is not asserted as a standalone disciplinary policy.
Approved because the Provost guidance directly states WSU does not endorse AI detection tools and will not allow detectors as sole support for academic misconduct cases.
Approved as an AI-related policy-reference claim from the WSU Provost page; the claim is limited to the source statement about EP8 and generative AI queries.
Approved as research-enterprise guidance, not as a general student course rule.
Approved as a narrow Office of Research guidance claim about attribution in research outputs and publications.

artifacts.title: Washington State University AI Policy Staged Findings
artifacts.summary: Staged review found official WSU Provost Office guidance on course AI expectations, AI-related data handling policy references, and academic misconduct reporting, plus Office of Research guidance for generative AI in research. No single central binding university-wide generative AI policy was found in the official sources reviewed; claims are scoped to Provost guidance, Provost policy-reference text, or Office of Research guidance and remain staged candidates.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No central binding university-wide generative AI policy was identified; claim wording is limited to official Provost guidance, Provost AI-related policy references, and Office of Research guidance.
Department-level or program-specific AI policy pages were rejected for university-wide claims.
No login-gated, paywalled, CAPTCHA-protected, or non-official sources were used.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
